# RedwoodSDK: Ping / Pong JSX Route ## Background RedwoodSDK (rwsdk) is a server-first React framework for the Cloudflare platform. It runs as a Vite plugin and lets you handle requests with `defineApp`, `render`, and `route` from the `rwsdk/worker` and `rwsdk/router` packages, where route handlers can return either a standard `Response` or JSX that is rendered to HTML on the server. A baseline RedwoodSDK project has already been scaffolded at `/home/user/myproject`. Your job is to add a new HTTP route to the existing app that returns JSX and is rendered as a real HTML page. ## Requirements - Add a new GET route at the path `/ping` to the existing RedwoodSDK app in `src/worker.tsx`. - The `/ping` route must return a JSX element (not a raw `new Response(...)`), so that rwsdk renders it as a full HTML document via the `Document` shell. - The returned JSX must contain the literal text `Pong` wrapped inside an HTML element (for example `

Pong

`). - The existing routes (such as `/`) and the `Document` shell must continue to work; do not break the existing app. - The project must still run under the standard dev server command (`npm run dev`) on the default Vite port `5173`. ## Implementation Hints - Look at `src/worker.tsx` and observe how the existing app uses `defineApp`, `render`, and `route` from `rwsdk/worker` / `rwsdk/router`. - Add a new `route("/ping", ...)` entry inside the same `render(Document, [...])` block so the new route also goes through the shared HTML document shell. - Route handlers can return JSX directly; RedwoodSDK will render it to HTML on the server. No client-side hydration is required for this task. - You do not need to add any new dependencies, change `wrangler.jsonc`, or modify the `Document` component. ## Acceptance Criteria - Project path: `/home/user/myproject` - Start command: `npm run dev` - Port: `5173` - API Endpoints / Routes: - `GET /ping`: - Returns HTTP status `200`. - Response `Content-Type` indicates HTML (for example `text/html`). - Response body is an HTML document whose body contains the literal text `Pong` wrapped in an HTML element (such as `

Pong

`, `

Pong

`, `

Pong

`, etc.). Plain text responses or bodies that contain only the bare string `Pong` without an enclosing HTML element are NOT acceptable. - `GET /` continues to respond with HTTP status `200` (the original route behavior must be preserved).

{
  "name": "@redwoodjs/starter",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "A bare-bones RedwoodSDK starter",
  "main": "index.js",
  "type": "module",
  "keywords": [],
  "author": "",
  "license": "MIT",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "build": "vite build",
    "dev": "vite dev",
    "dev:init": "rw-scripts dev-init",
    "preview": "vite preview",
    "worker:run": "rw-scripts worker-run",
    "clean": "npm run clean:vite",
    "clean:vite": "rm -rf ./node_modules/.vite",
    "release": "rw-scripts ensure-deploy-env && npm run clean && npm run build && wrangler deploy",
    "generate": "rw-scripts ensure-env && wrangler types --include-runtime false",
    "check": "npm run generate && npm run types",
    "types": "tsc"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "react": "19.2.6",
    "react-dom": "19.2.6",
    "react-server-dom-webpack": "19.2.6",
    "rwsdk": "1.2.11"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@cloudflare/vite-plugin": "1.33.2",
    "@cloudflare/workers-types": "4.20260426.1",
    "@types/node": "~25.6.0",
    "@types/react": "19.2.14",
    "@types/react-dom": "19.2.3",
    "typescript": "6.0.3",
    "vite": "~7.3.2",
    "wrangler": "4.85.0"
  },
  "pnpm": {
    "onlyBuiltDependencies": [
      "esbuild",
      "sharp",
      "workerd"
    ]
  }
}

export const Document: React.FC<{ children: React.ReactNode }> = ({
  children,
}) => (
  <html lang="en">
    <head>
      <meta charSet="utf-8" />
      <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
      <title>@redwoodjs/starter-minimal</title>
      <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com" />
      <link
        rel="preconnect"
        href="https://fonts.gstatic.com"
        crossOrigin=""
      />
      <link
        rel="stylesheet"
        href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Noto+Sans:ital,wght@0,100..900;1,100..900&family=Playfair+Display:ital,wght@0,400..900;1,400..900&display=optional"
        precedence="first"
      />
      <link rel="modulepreload" href="/src/client.tsx" />
    </head>
    <body>
      {children}
      <script>import("/src/client.tsx")</script>
    </body>
  </html>
);

part.text:
**Analyzing Component Structure**

I've identified that the `Document` component is instrumental in generating the complete HTML structure, encompassing `<html>`, `<head>`, and `<body>` tags. This is crucial for understanding how content, like the `/ping` endpoint's "Pong" heading and the client script, is integrated into the final rendered page.

**Verifying JSX Dependencies**

I'm currently scrutinizing `src/worker.tsx` and its imports, specifically investigating whether React needs to be explicitly imported when JSX is utilized. My focus is on ensuring that the compilation and bundling process correctly handles this, especially given that `src/app/document.tsx` uses `React.FC` without an explicit React import.

**Clarifying JSX Imports**

I'm currently analyzing the implications of using `"react-jsx"` versus `React.FC`. My thoughts are focusing on whether the presence of `React.FC` in files like `src/app/document.tsx` necessitates a React import, even with the `"react-jsx"` transform enabled.

todos.content: Add route for GET /ping in src/worker.tsx returning JSX containing literal text "Pong" wrapped inside an HTML element
Start the development server and verify both GET / and GET /ping work correctly

**Refining the code**

I'm currently re-examining `src/worker.tsx`, considering an update with `applyDiff`. My focus is on integrating the import statements for `render`, `route` from `rwsdk/router`, and `defineApp` from `rwsdk/worker`.

**Analyzing the Server Response**

I'm currently processing the server's successful HTTP 200 OK response. The content type is confirmed as HTML, and I'm examining the structure within the `hydrate-root` div, noting the presence of the "Pong" heading and the `rwsdk-app-end` element, all within a complete HTML document structure.

input.result:
I have successfully added the requested GET `/ping` route that returns JSX to the RedwoodSDK application. ### Changes Made: - Modified `home/user/myproject/src/worker.tsx` to add a new `route("/ping", ...)` inside the `render(Document, [...])` block: ```typescript render(Document, [ route("/", Home), route("/ping", () =>

Pong

), ]), ``` - Verified that the route successfully renders through the shared `Document` shell to output a complete HTML document. - Started and tested the development server (`npm run dev` on port `5173`) to confirm: - `GET /` responds with `200 OK` and the original welcome page. - `GET /ping` responds with `200 OK`, a `Content-Type` of `text/html`, and a response body containing `

Pong

` wrapped inside the `Document` HTML shell.
